title: lopezia racemosa subsp. racemosa
intro: Lopezia racemosa subsp. racemosa is a subspecies of flowering plant in the evening primrose family, known for its fragrant, white, and star-shaped flowers that grow in clusters. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. Lopezia racemosa subsp. racemosa is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating respiratory issues, and promoting digestion, as well as having antioxidant and antiseptic effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be dried and used as a powder for medicinal purposes.
